response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer, but their profile is centered on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep C++ expertise this role requires. Their only professional C++ experience was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and there is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial automation domain experience. The systems-level DLang work shows transferable low-latency skills, but the gap to a senior C++ role in industrial cybersecurity is too large.
strengths: Strong systems-level experience with low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of requests/sec (DLang at Dunnhumby)
Broad polyglot background with solid engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development)
Experience building event-driven, distributed platforms (GISCollective on Kubernetes) plus mentoring/teaching track record
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — only a short 2014-2015 role; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or industrial automation protocols
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not high-performance C++ or industrial/embedded systems

key_factors.factor: Transferable low-latency and event-driven systems experience
